core_mechanism: coherent two-state oscillation/conversion between two coupled propagating modes traversing an external field (avoided-level-crossing mixing), producing an energy-dependent conversion probability that imprints oscillatory 'absorption-like' features on the transmitted spectrum
brief_reason: The signal arises from a coupling between two species (ALPs and photons) that induces oscillations in an external magnetic field and thereby imprints distinctive energy-dependent spectral irregularities, which is exactly a two-mode coherent mixing/conversion process, with an ensemble-stacking average over source pairs used only to stabilize the predicted pattern.
